title: [packit] Propose downstream failed for release v1.31.1
body:
Packit failed on creating pull-requests in dist-git (https://src.fedoraproject.org/rpms/buildah.git):

| dist-git branch | error |
| --------------- | ----- |
| `f37` | See https://dashboard.packit.dev/results/propose-downstream/2978 |
| `f38` | See https://dashboard.packit.dev/results/propose-downstream/2976 |
| `rawhide` | See https://dashboard.packit.dev/results/propose-downstream/2977 |


You can retrigger the update by adding a comment (`/packit propose-downstream`) into this issue.

comments.body: @lsm5 PTAL, not sure what actions do we need to take here.

@flouthoc so the `rpm/update-spec-provides.sh` script failed in packit tasks. I had sent a patch to main branch to get rid of that script https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/4926 but I didn't cherry-pick it to release-1.31. Doing it now.
Will be fixed by https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/4963 . But we can only verify it in the next upstream release. So, I'll close this.
